turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Role Play: Discussing Group Work Roles with a Teammate

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, let’s do the role play. You’re my teammate. I want to talk about our group project.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure. What do you want to discuss about the project?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I feel like we haven’t really decided who does which part yet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yeah, I feel the same. Everything feels a bit unclear. What do you think we should do first?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think we should assign roles, so everyone knows their responsibility.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds reasonable. Do you already know which role you’d prefer?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I’m comfortable doing research and writing, but I’m not confident with presenting.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, so you’d rather not present. Do you mean you don’t want to present at all?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Not exactly. I mean… I can present if needed, but I prefer not to.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. Thanks for explaining. You could say that you’re happy to help, but that research and writing are your strengths.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, that sounds better than just saying no.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: We could also suggest a small deadline so the project doesn’t get delayed.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: When you say small deadline, do you mean this week or next week?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I was thinking by the end of next week.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So you’ll suggest roles and a deadline, and we’ll see how the group responds.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes. I feel more confident about talking to them now.
